name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical method development, quality control, and materials characterization. Expertise in HPLC/UPLC, spectroscopy (FT-IR, UV-Vis), thermal analysis (TGA/DSC), and chromatographic methods for small molecule and peptide analysis. Strong background in DOE, method validation, and GMP documentation. Experienced in developing robust analytical methods and supporting cross-functional development teams.

education.details: Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

experience.role: Postdoctoral Researcher — Analytical Development & Process Optimization
bullets: Develop and validate analytical methods for small molecule and polymer characterization using HPLC, GPC/SEC, FT-IR, and thermal analysis.
Apply DOE approaches to optimize analytical methods and establish control strategies for process development.
Support quality control activities including stability studies, method qualification, and documentation.
Developed and validated analytical methods for small molecule characterization using HPLC, NMR, GC-MS, and spectroscopic techniques.
Conducted comprehensive analytical characterization including purity assessment, impurity profiling, and stability studies.
Authored technical documentation and maintained detailed electronic lab notebooks in compliance with research best practices.

skills.Analytical: HPLC/UPLC, GC, GPC/SEC, method development, validation, transfer
skills.Quality: cGMP awareness, stability programs, method qualification, documentation
skills.Data: DOE, SPC, Origin, JMP, MestReNova, ChemDraw
